extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won by waiting until Ackermann faded after going too early from the final corner 250m out, isolated but with superior kick.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe controlled late but Ackermann launched 250m too early from final corner, allowing Ewan superior kick to prevail.
GC teams moved to front with 10km remaining for descent positioning, fragmenting sprint trains prematurely before final kilometers.
Viviani repeatedly ignored own leadout from Ciccone/Seneschal, choosing to follow Ewan's wheel instead, negating QuickStep numerical advantage.
Dimension Data kept Ewan isolated but positioned through penultimate corner, trusting his acceleration over traditional full leadout train.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: admitted feeling worse than expected on 'harder than thought' finale but superior kick overcame positioning disadvantage
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: had full Bora train support but tactical error launching from 250m corner rather than holding for shorter sprint
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: deliberately abandoned own QuickStep leadout twice to follow Ewan's wheel, suggesting lack of confidence in teammates or form
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: neutral: publicly frustrated with breakaway companions' contributions at KOM sprint, revealing leadership expectations within jersey competitions
